close_reason: Inline tests cover unit-level cache behavior; integration tests cover end-to-end. Overlap is intentional.
description:
src/lib.rs:136-239 contains a #[cfg(test)] mod tests with four tests:
  cache_hit_does_not_call_inner
  zero_ttl_always_calls_inner
  put_populates_cache
  refresh_updates_cache

tests/caching_store.rs contains five integration tests covering the same scenarios with the same scaffolding pattern (a counting mock SecretStore):
  cache_hit_within_ttl
  cache_miss_after_ttl_expiry          ← only one not duplicated inline
  ttl_zero_never_caches
  refresh_bypasses_cache
  put_updates_cache

The two test modules independently define a near-identical mock backend (FakeStore vs MockStore), with different counter primitives (AtomicUsize vs Arc<Mutex<usize>>), different value types (&'static [u8] vs String), and different test names — telling the same story twice with cosmetic divergence.

The integration tests in tests/caching_store.rs are the right home for this crate's tests because they exercise CachingStore through its public API only (no super:: imports, no private fields touched). Keeping a duplicate inline mod adds maintenance burden — every behavior change requires updating both, and they have already drifted (only the tests/ version covers TTL expiry).

Fix: delete src/lib.rs:136-239. Move cache_miss_after_ttl_expiry coverage to tests/caching_store.rs (already present there). Drop the dev-dependency on async_trait inside src — it remains a real dependency since the trait impl uses it.

Alternative (rejected): keep both with a comment. Two test modules exercising the same surface with the same mock pattern is dead duplication regardless of intent.
title: Inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests duplicates tests/caching_store.rs scenarios
